Utility functions¶

This module serves as a collection for methods not strictly limited to a singular application case.

source_reconstruction.utils.format_fiducials(pre_mri_ref_pts)¶

Transforms an mne-fiducials object into an dictionary containing the fiducials.

Parameters:

pre_mri_ref_pts – The mne-fiducials object in question.

Returns:

Dictionary containing the fiducial points.

source_reconstruction.utils.run_subprocess_in_custom_working_directory(subject_id, cmd)¶

Creates a custom work directory to run a freesurfer command within.

Parameters:

  • subject_id – Name of the subject whose data is worked on.

  • cmd – The freesurfer command to be executed in the custom environment.

source_reconstruction.utils.init_fs_paths(subjects_path)¶

Runs some freesurfer initialization steps.

Parameters:

subjects_path – Path to the subject folder.

source_reconstruction.utils.fast_cross_product(a, b)¶

Calculates the cross product between two vectors. Seemingly numpy has too much overhead here to be fast.

Parameters:

  • a – The 1st vector in the cross product.

  • b – The 2nd vector in the cross product.

Returns:

Crossproduct of vectors a x b.

source_reconstruction.utils.norm_vert(vert)¶

Normalizes a vector. Seemingly numpy has too much overhead here to be fast.

Parameters:

vert – The to be normalized vector.

Returns:

The normalized vector.

source_reconstruction.utils.find_nearest_neighbor(src_model, tgt_pt, method='kdtree')¶

Employs two methods to find the nearest neighbors. In case a src_model a list of points is provided, a model is trained, otherwise, pretrained models are used if a (KDTree or BallTree objects) are provided.

Parameters:

  • src_model – Either a list of data points or a kdtree/balltree object.

  • tgt_pt – The tgt_point to be queried.

Returns:

The nearest neighbor of the respective point.

source_reconstruction.utils.find_valid_vertices(mri_vert, model_vert, max_neighborhood_size=5)¶

Matches freesurfer reconstructed mri vertices (sphere) with model vertices (octahedron).

Parameters:

  • mri_vert – Freesurfer based vertices (sphere).

  • vert (model) – Octahedron based vertices.

Returns:

Binary list of Freesurfer vertices that have a match in the model vertices (octahedron).

source_reconstruction.utils.magn_of_vec(vec)¶

Calculates the magnitude of a vector

Parameters:

vec – The vector in question.

Returns:

The magnitude of vec.

source_reconstruction.utils.apply_inv_transformation(data, trans)¶

Applies the inverse of trans to data.

Parameters:

  • data – The data to be transformed.

  • trans – The transformation matrix (4x4 matrix; rotation + translation only).

Returns:

The transformed data.

source_reconstruction.utils.calc_quat_angle(a, b)¶

Calculate the angle between two quaternions.

Parameters:

  • a – The 1st quaternion.

  • b – The 2nd quaternion.

Returns:

The angle between a and b.

source_reconstruction.utils.orient_mat_to_block_format(orient_mat)¶

Transforms an orientation matrix (rotation matrix) into (sparse) block format.

Parameters:

orient_mat – The orientation matrix in question.

Returns:

The transformed orientation matrix.

source_reconstruction.utils.get_eigenbasis(vortex_normals, valid_vert, cluster_grp, cluster_indices, mri_to_head_trans, double_precision=40)¶

Calculates an orthonormal basis of eigenvector/values for each supporting/valid point.

Parameters:

  • vortex_normals – Normals of the supporting vertices.

  • valid_vert – List of valid/supporting vertices.

  • cluster_grp – Clusters represented by a single vortex.

  • cluster_indices – Cluster indices.

  • mri_to_head_trans – Transformation from MRI to head coordinates.

  • double_precision – Numerical precision of the eigenvectors/values (default: 40 digits).

Returns:

Orthonormal eigenbasis.

source_reconstruction.utils.calc_acc_hem_normals(geom_white_vert, geom_white_faces)¶

Generatoes vertex normals from accumulated face normals. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vertex_normal.

Parameters:

  • geom_white_vert – Array of vertices.

  • geom_white_faces – Array of faces.

Returns:

Array of surface normals for the input faces/vertices.

source_reconstruction.utils.find_vertex_clusters(geom_white_vert, valid_geom_vert, geom_white_faces)¶

Identifies which input vertices (geom_white_vert) are presented by which valid vortex.

Parameters:

  • geom_white_vert – To be clustered vertices.

  • valid_geom_vert – Binary list of valid vertices.

  • geom_white_faces – Corresponding faces.

Returns:

(cluster_grp, cluster_indices) - Information on clusters.

source_reconstruction.utils.cortical_surface_reorient_fwd_model(lh_geom_white_vert, lh_geom_white_faces, valid_geom_lh_vert, rh_geom_white_vert, rh_geom_white_faces, valid_geom_rh_vert, fwd_sol, mri_to_head_trans)¶

Transforms a fwd model into surface orientation (orthogonal to the respective surface cluster; allowing for cortically constrained inverse modeling) and shrinks it by making closeby channels project to the same destination. This is different from a ‘default’ 3D transformation.

Parameters:

  • lh_geom_white_vert – Lh vertices.

  • lh_geom_white_faces – Lh faces.

  • valid_geom_lh_vert – Valid/supporting lh vertices.

  • rh_geom_white_vert – Rh vertices.

  • rh_geom_white_faces – Rh faces.

  • valid_geom_rh_vert – Valid/supporting rh vertices.

  • fwd_sol – Previous forward solution.

  • mri_to_head_trans – MRI to head transformation matrix.

Returns:

Transformed surface model.

source_reconstruction.utils.calc_mri_maps(subj_path, fs_avg_path, hemisphere, overwrite)¶

Find the subject space points corresponding to fs avg space points.

Parameters:

  • subj_path – Path to subject freesurfer files.

  • fs_avg_path – Path fo fs average freesurfer files.

  • hemisphere – hemisphere to compute for.

  • overwrite – Flag whether to overwrite preexisting mri maps.

Returns:

source_reconstruction.utils.calc_small_to_default_vertices_proj(valid_vert, sub_vert, sub_faces)¶

Identifies

source_reconstruction.utils.get_mri_subj_to_fs_avg_trans_mat(valid_lh_vert, valid_rh_vert, model_vert, subj_path, fs_avg_path, overwrite)¶

Create a subject to fs average transformation matrix.

Parameters:

  • valid_lh_vert – Valid lh vertices (subject space).

  • valid_rh_vert – Valid rh vertices (subject space).

  • model_vert – Model vertices.

  • subj_path – Path to subject’s freesurfer files.

  • fs_avg_path – Path to fs average’s freesurfer files.

  • overwrite – Flag whether to overwrite MRI maps.

Returns:

(trans_mat, valid_avg_lh_vert, valid_avg_rh_vert) - Transformation matrix, valid/supporting vertices for left & right hemisphere

source_reconstruction.utils.apply_mri_subj_to_fs_avg_trans_mat(trans_mat, data)¶

Transforms data from subject space to fs_average space

Parameters:

  • trans_mat – Transformation from subject to fs average space.

  • data – Data in subject space.

Returns:

Data in fs avg space.

source_reconstruction.utils.calc_whitener(eigen_val, eigen_vec)¶

Calculate PCA based whitener from provided eigenvalues and eigenvectors.

Parameters:

  • eigen_val – Eigenvalues.

  • eigen_vec – Eigenvectors.

Returns:

Whitener.

previous

Boundary element model (BEM)

next
